title: Google DeepMind funds research into risks from large-scale agent interaction
friendlyTitle: DeepMind is studying what happens when many AI agents interact
summary: MIT Technology Review reports that Google DeepMind and partners are backing a $10 million research effort into multi-agent AI safety. The focus is on what happens when many autonomous agents interact online, including risks around scams, prompt injection, cyberattacks, and unpredictable collective behavior.
friendlySummary: Google DeepMind and partners are funding research into risks that could appear when millions of AI agents interact online. The concerns include scams, prompt injection, cyberattacks, and unpredictable group behavior.
whyItMatters: Agent safety is quickly becoming operational rather than theoretical. Businesses deploying agents will need practical guardrails for interaction, permissions, identity, and abuse prevention before agent-to-agent workflows become commonplace.
friendlyWhyItMatters: As agents become common, they will not work in isolation. People and companies need rules and safety tools before agent-to-agent activity becomes part of the internet’s plumbing.
